name: Voltyran
worldsense: [Origon Yellow-gem creature] Voltyran is an early Yellow-gem creature of Origon, the Ancient Forge World — a living storm shaped by the forge-world's raw elemental power, bearing three naturally formed yellow gems within his body. He is not an Immortal. Born from Elzoran (the canonical Elzoran-line reference from his original card line), he stands among the gem-beings native to Origon — the planet whose atmosphere and core saturate every living thing with naturally formed gems, and whose people guard the Father Gem at Origon's heart.
card_text:
Voltyran
Tier VI Pseudoimmortal · Creature / Beast
HP 374 · ATK 450 · DEF 324 · SPD 525 · SPC 325  (Total 1998)

A1 — Static Impulsivity — 1 gem · 33% ATK ~148 damage
It strikes before it has finished deciding to — a living storm has no patience, and the bolt is in the foe before the thunder agrees to follow.

A2 — Voltagious Rend — 2 gems · inflicts Undershock
It rends the foe with a current drawn from the divine lightning that remade it, and where the charge settles, one of their strengths is scorched dim.

A3 — Thunderfall Execution — 3 gems · 99% ATK ~446 damage & Undershock
It calls the whole sky down in a single fall of light, and the foe sees the flash — all they will see, because the thunder arrives too late.

Those who see the lightning rarely live to hear the thunder.

name: Abyssion
worldsense: [Cosmic Guardian — Tier IX] Abyssion, 'The Dimensional Maw' — Guardian of Collapse. Where Alphaea creates order, Abyssion governs destruction: he consumes unstable dimensions before their corruption can spread across the Expanse, devouring broken realities, failed timelines, dimensional infections and unstable astralite zones. Entire worlds have vanished within his jaws. His body is a cosmic dragon of fractured reality, scaled in black, red and blue — the architecture of collapse made flesh. Terrible, but necessary — without collapse, reality would accumulate corruption until existence itself became unsustainable. — In the Cosmic Mirror Cycle, Abyssion governs Quadrant III, the Trinity Zone (--): the collapse state, where only body, mind and spirit remain. — Lineage: one of the four dracolords, born of Azyrath from gathered space matter — among the six survivors of the Aenor Eruption's great dying, and a founder of the Eternal Accord.
card_text:
Abyssion
Tier IX Demigod · Ultramax / Unknown-Void
HP 600 · ATK 666 · DEF 555 · SPD 456 · SPC 720  (Total 2997)

A1 — Maw Breach — 1 gem · 33% ATK ~220 damage
His jaws part on a seam of failing reality, and the foe is bitten by the plain fact that some things are not meant to keep existing.

A2 — Entropy Spiral — 2 gems · inflicts Souldrift
He marks the foe as an unstable dimension, and entropy answers the mark — they begin to come apart, quietly, the way failed realities do.

A3 — Dimensional Maw — 3 gems · 99% ATK ~659 damage & Souldrift
He opens fully, and the foe is drawn down the throat of collapse — to the place broken worlds go, and from which nothing returns.

Without collapse, reality would gather corruption until existence itself became unsustainable.

name: Abyssylyx
worldsense: The Immortals do not command him; they trust that nothing crawls out of Eastern darks unannounced.
card_text:
Abyssylyx
Tier VI Pseudoimmortal · Aura / Beast
HP 372 · ATK 396 · DEF 330 · SPD 432 · SPC 468  (Total 1998)

A1 — Tidefang Strike — 1 gem · 33% ATK ~131 damage
He moves the way deep water moves — without warning and without sound — the wound is already open before the coastline has begun to sink.

A2 — Mesmercoil — 2 gems · inflicts Brainlock
His eyes are two coils with no ending, and the foe who meets them forgets the shape of their own intent, left holding nothing but struggle.

A3 — Worldchoke Descent — 3 gems · 99% ATK ~392 damage & Brainlock
Abyssylyx loops the world in one slow coil and draws it under — down to the sunless deep, where thought runs too dark to ever surface again.

The Immortals do not command him; they trust that nothing crawls out of Eastern darks unannounced.

name: Anciuxor
worldsense: Anciuxor is the physical manifestation of the Highest One within the lower realms of existence. It does not rule through emotion or conquest, but through absolute cosmic correction and balance. Appearing only during Cosmic Crises, it stands above Immortals, Gemlords, and all known forms of life. As the All-Seeing Gatekeeper and the Father, Anciuxor sits cleanly at Tier X — witness and overseer of the Eternal Accord, standing above the six Dracolord Guardians though he is not a Dracolord himself. His card is a one-of-one, Creator-owned: the single rarest card in the TCG, and is never to be reprinted.
card_text:
Anciuxor
Tier X God · Ultramax / Ultramax
HP 666 · ATK 666 · DEF 666 · SPD 666 · SPC 666  (Total 3330)

A1 — Divine Verdict — 1 gem · 33% ATK ~220 damage
It does not strike the foe so much as rule on them — and the sentence, once spoken, carries the weight of a thing that was always going to be true.

A2 — Realm Severance — 2 gems · inflicts Souldrift
It draws a line between the foe and the realm that lent them substance, and on the wrong side of that line they quietly begin to be subtracted.

A3 — Divine Transplacement — 3 gems · 99% ATK ~659 damage & Souldrift
It moves the foe from the column of things that exist to the column of things corrected, and the cosmos closes the gap behind them without comment.

Anciuxor does not rule through emotion or conquest, but through absolute cosmic correction.

name: Andre Hart
worldsense: Andre Hart — a regular guy raised by good adoptive parents (the inverse of the Harry Potter guardian trope), and the last carrier of the Veridae line — the hidden AUR- bloodline that UMBRIS and Khronis hunt for experimentation. Both his biological parents were captured by UMBRIS; the orphan placement was no accident — Lucienis, hiding as 'Old Louis' the record-shop owner Andre's adoptive parents patronized, shepherded the heir in plain sight, and Andre grew up among the vinyls without ever being told what he was. A fan of Valstorm and The Mech, he pursued Astratech as an intern, rose record-store manager → Astratech operative → was recruited directly by Valstorm by the end of the Third War; became Keeper of the Book of Viridia and mastered it, ascended to A4, and discovered he was the lost AUR-. He survived where every other carrier died (the living taken alive for experimentation, the rest 'overcooked' by untrained latent A4 potential) because belonging and normalcy kept him below UMBRIS's radar — the same hunt that took his parents. After winning the Third War he rejects the throne and becomes Aurexion. His Viridian-stage powers manipulate vector force, spatial momentum and Astral synchronization — predicting opposing action and striking before formations stabilize.
card_text:
Andre Hart
Tier IV Legend · Humanoid-Noid / Aura
HP 240 · ATK 305 · DEF 227 · SPD 267 · SPC 293  (Total 1332)

A1 — Vector Drive — 1 gem · 33% ATK ~101 damage
He is already where the foe will need him not to be — the strike lands on a prediction, and the foe arrives late to their own defense.

A2 — Astral Projection — 2 gems · inflicts Brainlock
He steps out of his own position and into the foe's intention, and a mind that finds him sharing the room can no longer plan in private — or at all.

A3 — Divine Intuition — 3 gems · 99% ATK ~302 damage & Brainlock
He has read every move the foe still believes is theirs to make, and answers all of them in a single motion, before the first has begun.

Andre Hart dominates battlefields by predicting enemy actions and striking before formations can stabilize.

name: Audrellius
worldsense: Audrellius is the standard-bearer of the modern humanoid race — the figure all living humanity looks to. The true origin of humankind lies with Egnellahc, Humanoid Prime, but the deep cosmic history of the ancients has been redacted and all but lost; few mortals have ever heard the Prime's name. To the modern world, Audrellius is humanity's icon: spoken of as the next great ascension of mortalkind, the closest any mortal has come to echoing what Egnellahc once was.
card_text:
Audrellius
Tier V Apex · Humanoid-Noid / Aura
HP 366 · ATK 333 · DEF 366 · SPD 300 · SPC 300  (Total 1665)

A1 — Vanguard Charge — 1 gem · 33% ATK ~110 damage
He never trained for war, yet reads it as his bloodline always will — a step ahead already, the unified front charging in on his call.

A2 — Imperial Mandate — 2 gems · inflicts Brainlock
He speaks the mandate in Olden, and the old tongue does not ask — a will that hears the order carried in it forgets it was ever free to refuse.

A3 — Reign of Unity — 3 gems · 99% ATK ~330 damage & Brainlock
He raises Inventio's Worldstaff, and the whole unified world answers through it — law, crown and people as one weight nothing stands beneath.

Audrelius X did not conquer Viridia so much as unite it — the Order of the People is the world his victory built.

name: Aurellyn
worldsense: Aurellyn descended from Aurexion as inheritance descends from a name spoken once and never repeated — quietly, completely, without the world being asked. The chest-gem is not an ornament but a fragment of the father, given as a duty rather than a gift, and the shards that orbit him are pieces of his own becoming. He does not seek his throne; he sits where it appears, and the cathedral arranges itself around him.
card_text:
Aurellyn
Tier VI Pseudoimmortal · Humanoid-Noid / Aura
HP 408 · ATK 352 · DEF 344 · SPD 399 · SPC 495  (Total 1998)

A1 — Shardstorm Halo — 1 gem · 33% ATK ~116 damage
The shards orbiting him are pieces of his own becoming, and he spends a few without looking — a halo briefly thrown, the foe left standing inside it.

A2 — Auragold Verdict — 2 gems · inflicts Brainlock
He does not argue; he rules — and a mind told, with a father's certainty, exactly what it may do next is left only the smallest of permissions.

A3 — Aurelleap Max — 3 gems · 99% ATK ~348 damage & Brainlock
He rises to the height the inheritance always pointed toward and comes down as the whole of it — the gift, the duty, and the name landing together.

He does not seek his throne; he sits where it appears, and the cathedral arranges itself around him.

name: Aurexion
worldsense: Aurexion is what Andre Hart became. The bookshop kid and aspiring Astralnaut — a gifted Viridian who read vector force and spatial momentum better than anyone of his world — crossed the threshold no mortal was meant to cross and ascended. Where Andre predicted an opponent, Aurexion reads the motion of the Aethryx Expanse itself, synchronized with the Astral and no longer bound to any single planet. The transformation from Viridian to Astralnaut is the hinge of his story: the moment a mortal's potential stopped being potential and became something cosmic.
card_text:
Aurexion
Tier V Apex · Humanoid-Noid / Aura
HP 333 · ATK 333 · DEF 333 · SPD 333 · SPC 333  (Total 1665)

A1 — Starlance — 1 gem · 33% ATK ~110 damage
He no longer crosses a battlefield so much as a distance — the strike arrives down a line drawn star to star, and the foe is simply on it.

A2 — Cosmic Foresight — 2 gems · inflicts Brainlock
He reads not the foe's next move but every move the Expanse still leaves them, and a mind shown all its paths already walked finds none of its own.

A3 — Astral Synchrony — 3 gems · 99% ATK ~330 damage & Brainlock
For a moment he syncs with the whole Astral, and the foe is caught in a cosmos that answers his intuition — every exit closing as they reach it.

Andre Hart was the Viridian; Aurexion is the Astralnaut he became — the same mind, handed the whole sky to read.

name: Diviniara
worldsense: Her crystal feathers were not grown — they are what remains when divine light illuminates ancient ice to ascend the skies.
card_text:
Diviniara
Tier VI Pseudoimmortal · Aura / Beast
HP 420 · ATK 348 · DEF 402 · SPD 348 · SPC 480  (Total 1998)

A1 — Halocrest Slash — 1 gem · 33% ATK ~115 damage
A wing of frozen light parts the air before the air, the temperature drops before shivers remember being born.

A2 — Auroral Blizzard — 2 gems · inflicts Gridfreeze
The light from her crest pours over the foe and simply stops — and what the light has touched forgets that motion is a privilege.

A3 — Eternal Halo Eclipse — 3 gems · 99% ATK ~345 damage & Gridfreeze
The aurora folds into a single frozen sun, then darkens. For one silent breath the world is ice — and ice has never learned how to move.

Her crystal feathers were not grown — they are what remains when divine light fills ancient ice to ascend the skies.

name: Egnellahc
worldsense: Egnellahc, Humanoid Prime — the first humanoid and the origin of all humanoid life across the Aethryx Expanse. He arose from the Elzoran line, the purified royal Mandrake bloodline of Elzoran, and did not seek power but transcendence; his Transplacement Verse shattered the world of Nexyros and scattered humanity across the Expanse, seeding every humanoid lineage that would ever exist. Every Humanoid-Noid across the Aethryx Expanse traces its bloodline to him. What remained of his sundered mind ascended into the Gemlords Oathane and Oatheus — making him father to both a race and two Immortals.
card_text:
Egnellahc
Tier VII Pseudoimmortal · Humanoid-Noid / Spirit
HP 407 · ATK 291 · DEF 525 · SPD 525 · SPC 583  (Total 2331)

A1 — Fractured Verse — 1 gem · 33% ATK ~96 damage
He speaks one broken line of the Verse that once shattered a world, and even a fragment lands like a sentence the foe was always going to be inside.

A2 — Astral Suppression — 2 gems · inflicts Brainlock
He presses on the part of the foe that reaches outward — the planning, the wanting, the next idea — and holds it shut, leaving them only instinct.

A3 — Mind Absolute — 3 gems · 99% ATK ~288 damage & Brainlock
For one moment his sundered mind is whole again, and a whole mind set against a single foe leaves them nothing of their own thought to stand on.

Every Humanoid-Noid that walks Viridia traces its bloodline back to him.

name: Elzoran
worldsense: Known as the Supreme Dragon Lord, Elzoran commands all elements, summoning eclipses that warp the sky itself. He was once consumed by a shadow aura that twisted him into the catastrophic entity Omegoran — until Anciuxor itself purged the corruption, restoring the dragon to his true form. Elzoran's awakening marks not battle, but the memory of what he nearly became.
card_text:
Elzoran
Tier V Apex · Beast / Nature
HP 366 · ATK 333 · DEF 266 · SPD 234 · SPC 466  (Total 1665)

A1 — Elemental Eclipse — 1 gem · 33% ATK ~110 damage
He calls a brief eclipse down over the foe alone, and every element answers at once — the foe struck by all of them agreeing, for a moment, to be one.

A2 — Telekenetic Rend — 2 gems · inflicts Gridfreeze
He closes one claw on nothing, and the sky over the foe goes dark and still; caught in an eclipse of his making, they cannot find the moment to move.

A3 — Helm of Chaos — 3 gems · 99% ATK ~330 damage & Gridfreeze
He wears the chaos that once consumed him like a chosen crown — the catastrophe Omegoran was, brought down now by the dragon who survived it.

Elzoran's awakening marks not a battle, but the memory of what he nearly became.

name: Khronis
worldsense: [Daemon Prime] The architect of all engineered corruption in Viridia — Malrec Malviros, the ancient Daemon Prime called Khronis, who abandoned natural order for controlled evolution and absolute power. A towering winged humanoid of pale hardened flesh and crown-curved horns, veins lit with corrupted astral energy; fully sentient, calculating and methodical, he regards the world as a system to be rewritten, and is the creator of the Morispore lineage and the force behind every hybrid and ritual transformation. In the modern era he operates through Khronis Corporation — daemon-cult corruption evolved into a Sith-like hidden corporate elite running 'Astral Capitalism,' profiting from the disasters it engineers. His true goal is controlled collapse, not money; he stays young by consuming the blood of powerful Astrals (the literal 'eat and drink people'), and has bought UMBRIS into a bargain that hunts the AUR- bloodline and other high Astrals on his behalf. Shown here in his A4 Awakening — the Khronicore Endgame Sacrifice — where he consumes his own Armageddon Dragon entirely, tearing out its astral core and fusing dragon, Mori and daemon essence into raw corrupted density. The sky fractured, the ground ruptured, and the Veil weakened as the Underworld bled into Viridia. Khronis did not ascend with his dragon — he devoured it to become something greater.
card_text:
Khronis
Tier VI Pseudoimmortal · Beast / Unknown-Void
HP 510 · ATK 498 · DEF 360 · SPD 294 · SPC 336  (Total 1998)

A1 — Astralblight Lash — 1 gem · 33% ATK ~164 damage
The corrupted astral light beneath his skin answers the smallest thought, and it reaches the foe before he has decided they should be reached.

A2 — Morispore Rite — 2 gems · inflicts Souldrift
He works the rite over the foe that authored every Mori, and the corruption takes — rewriting them into something that no longer holds together.

A3 — Khronicore Sacrifice — 3 gems · 99% ATK ~493 damage & Souldrift
He tears the core from his own dragon and devours it whole, and the foe is caught in that ascension — the sky fracturing, the veil giving way.

He does not ascend beside his dragon — he devours it, to become the system he always meant the world to be.

name: Leonoror
worldsense: Trusted in maintaining virtue in the West; his third eye sees all things unholy and unjust in accordance with The Highest One.
card_text:
Leonoror
Tier VI Pseudoimmortal · Aura / Beast
HP 462 · ATK 444 · DEF 384 · SPD 336 · SPC 372  (Total 1998)

A1 — Sunclaw Eclipse — 1 gem · 33% ATK ~147 damage
He sets one paw down and the gold passes through what lay beneath — the West takes its tribute in a single unhurried stroke.

A2 — Vesper Roar — 2 gems · inflicts Souldrift
He opens his mouth and the sound comes out dusk-coloured — the foe hears the West in it, and something at their centre starts to leave.

A3 — Sunfall Reckoning — 3 gems · 99% ATK ~440 damage & Souldrift
Leonoror roars the sun down past the horizon and speaks the verdict into the gold dark — what was leaving the foe is now simply gone.

Trusted to keep virtue in the West; his third eye sees all things unholy and unjust, in accordance with The Highest One.

name: Lucienis
worldsense: [Virtue Immortal] Virtue Immortal of Humankind — among the oldest active immortal-class beings in Viridian history. The Highest One entrusted him with the Covenant of Humanity: protect their potential, not their choices. He is forbidden from domination or stripping free will and may only guide, warn, preserve, mentor and remember; restraint is his defining trait, and his great fear is humanity losing its ability to choose. He rules nothing, seeks no worship, and works through wisdom and quiet intervention — most often hidden as 'Old Louis,' a humble record-shop owner and historian (humility as philosophy) whose archive doubles as a Veil-sensitive safehouse. He reads the Veil and cosmic balance better than almost anyone alive, and recognized Khronis and Infernia's seepage long before the civilizations around him; across millennia he has shepherded the AUR- bloodline in secret, raising the orphaned heir Andre Hart right inside the record shop without ever telling him what he was. Lucienis walked beside humanity long before kingdoms learned to record their names — the living memory of their potential, believing humanity's greatest strength was never power but the ability to keep evolving despite its flaws.
card_text:
Lucienis
Tier VIII Immortal · Humanoid-Noid / Aura
HP 533 · ATK 533 · DEF 533 · SPD 533 · SPC 532  (Total 2664)

A1 — Memory Strike — 1 gem · 33% ATK ~176 damage
He calls up the long record of human survival, and the foe is met by every remembered hand at once — a whole species that learned how to endure.

A2 — Olden Script — 2 gems · inflicts Brainlock
He scripts a few lines of Olden into the air, and reality — long used to obeying the language — seals the cleverer half of the foe's mind shut.

A3 — Human Ascension — 3 gems · 99% ATK ~528 damage & Brainlock
For one moment he lets all of human potential through at once; it arrives as light, and even reality leans beneath a weight that large.

Not a god of power but the living memory of humankind — civilization remembering itself, refusing to be forgotten.

name: Natura
worldsense: [Virtue Immortal] Virtue Immortal of Nature — Mother Nature, who established every living system in Viridia and the ecological balance that holds them together. She shaped creatures, environments and the natural order, distributing life across land, sea and sky so that each form held a role in a larger ecosystem; survival became interconnected, and no species could stand entirely alone. Her design let life sustain itself without constant divine intervention — the natural order all beings depend on, and the balance that conflict and corruption forever threaten. Vice-paired with Vitriarch, Father of Corruption.
card_text:
Natura
Tier VIII Immortal · Nature / Creature
HP 666 · ATK 366 · DEF 633 · SPD 466 · SPC 533  (Total 2664)

A1 — Lifesurge — 1 gem · 33% ATK ~121 damage
She never strikes herself; the living world does it for her, and a surge of everything that grows rises to answer the foe in her place.

A2 — Worldroot Snare — 2 gems · inflicts Gridfreeze
She sends the world's roots up around the foe and folds them into the ecosystem — held, rooted, and left no turn in which to pull free.

A3 — Cradle of Creation — 3 gems · 99% ATK ~362 damage & Gridfreeze
She calls the whole interconnected web — land, sea and sky at once — and it closes over the foe like a cradle that has decided to keep them.

Natura is the natural order itself — the balance all life leans on, and the first thing corruption moves to break.

name: Omegoran
worldsense: Omegoran is Elzoran corrupted — the apex dragon after a shadow aura consumed him, fracturing his body beyond natural evolution into a catastrophic Unknown-class entity drifting between dimensions. He existed only as long as the corruption held: when Anciuxor purged the shadow aura, Omegoran unmade himself and Elzoran returned. He is the omen of what corruption can do to even the mightiest.
card_text:
Omegoran
Tier VII Pseudoimmortal · Beast / Unknown-Void
HP 493 · ATK 549 · DEF 383 · SPD 357 · SPC 549  (Total 2331)

A1 — Voidfang Guillotine — 1 gem · 33% ATK ~181 damage
Its fractured jaw comes down like a sentence with no appeal, and the void along the teeth takes far more than the bite alone could ever account for.

A2 — Cataclysmic Roar — 2 gems · inflicts Souldrift
It roars, and the sound is a piece of the corruption that made it — where the roar reaches the foe, something essential comes loose and drifts off.

A3 — The Final Armageddon — 3 gems · 99% ATK ~544 damage & Souldrift
It becomes, for one terrible moment, the catastrophe it was always going to be — and offers the foe the same unmaking Omegoran was, addressed to them.

Omegoran is the omen of what corruption can do to even the mightiest.

name: Ovauron
worldsense: [Tier IX Anomaly] Ovauron, 'The Wandering Hunger' — designation AE-28. The Anomaly of the Dichotomy Zone (Q2) of the Aethryx Expanse: a sentient 'drift planet,' a null world that carries no reality weight, adrift outside the cosmic order. It is a planet eater — it consumes lifeless worlds whole, and its slow drift now bends toward Earth. A one-off print: a single-copy card, never reprinted.
card_text:
Ovauron
Tier IX Demigod · Ultramax / Unknown-Void
HP 800 · ATK 666 · DEF 720 · SPD 300 · SPC 511  (Total 2997)

A1 — Drift Impact — 1 gem · 33% ATK ~220 damage
It does not charge; it arrives — the slow, certain drift of a whole dead world, and the foe is simply where it was always going to be.

A2 — Eternal Hunger — 2 gems · inflicts Souldrift
Its null gravity laps over the foe, and reality-weight begins to drain from them — the same slow unmaking it works on every dead world it swallows.

A3 — World Devour — 3 gems · 99% ATK ~659 damage & Souldrift
Ovauron opens, and the foe is taken the way a dead world is taken — pulled into the null at its core, and counted among the worlds that no longer are.

A sentient drift planet with no reality weight — it devours lifeless worlds, and its slow course bends toward Earth.

name: Phoenaris
worldsense: She does not rise because she was slain; she rises because the South must never be permitted to go cold.
card_text:
Phoenaris
Tier VI Pseudoimmortal · Aura / Beast
HP 366 · ATK 462 · DEF 312 · SPD 420 · SPC 438  (Total 1998)

A1 — Cinderlash — 1 gem · 33% ATK ~152 damage
A single feather slips its wing, and the air it crosses is already ash — the South does not strike, it remembers its own heat.

A2 — Emberbrand — 2 gems · inflicts Overcook
She sets a brand of living flame upon the foe; from that breath on they do not burn — they are burning, and the fire was never taught to stop.

A3 — Vermillion Resurrection — 3 gems · 99% ATK ~457 damage & Overcook
Phoenaris gives herself wholly to the pyre and climbs from her own ash once more — and all the blaze touched is kindled again as the new rises.

She does not rise because she was slain; she rises because the South must never be permitted to go cold.

name: Phrenetic
worldsense: Phrenetic is what Cerebrion would have become if it had ever felt the lust for power. The brain remains, the wit remains, the perception remains — but a fang grew where the wisdom should have, and the laughing began. Where Cerebrion sees probability, Phrenetic sees the version of every outcome in which something gets bitten.
card_text:
Phrenetic
Tier IV Legend · Unknown-Void / Spirit
HP 240 · ATK 218 · DEF 186 · SPD 288 · SPC 400  (Total 1332)

A1 — Skullbrain Pulse — 1 gem · 33% ATK ~72 damage
It sends a pulse from the exposed brain straight at the foe, and the thought inside it has a fang where a thought should not — and it bites going in.

A2 — Wraithgaze Inversion — 2 gems · inflicts Brainlock
It turns its gaze on the foe and inverts it — outside becomes in, plan becomes prey — a mind folded back to front does only what it cannot overthink.

A3 — Maniacal Reckoning — 3 gems · 99% ATK ~216 damage & Brainlock
It laughs as it has since the wisdom curdled, shows the foe every outcome in which something gets bitten, and then collects on all of them at once.

Where Cerebrion sees probability, Phrenetic sees the version of every outcome in which something gets bitten.

name: Royalesus
worldsense: He does not rule the world the Immortals left in his keeping; he holds high station, so that all beneath remembers there is refuge.
card_text:
Royalesus
Tier VI Pseudoimmortal · Aura / Beast
HP 432 · ATK 366 · DEF 360 · SPD 480 · SPC 360  (Total 1998)

A1 — Skylance — 1 gem · 33% ATK ~121 damage
His horn lowers once, and a thread of the high valleys goes with it — the foe is struck by a piece of the heavens before they have thought to look up.

A2 — Sovereign Verdict — 2 gems · inflicts Undershock
It regards the foe the way royalty regards all beneath them, and finds them yearning; under that gaze some part of them quietly agrees, and lessens.

A3 — Astral Coronation — 3 gems · 99% ATK ~362 damage & Undershock
He climbs until the stars consent to crown it, then descends as the whole weight of the sky — and what has already weakened in the fall crumbles.

He does not rule the realm the Immortals left him — he holds high station so all beneath remember there is refuge.

name: Sharkfin
worldsense: Sharkfin dominates the battlefield with a predator's instinct and a warmachine's precision. Its infrared beam sears through nearly all matter, while its jaws and tail unleash relentless destruction and storm-born chaos. Where it appears, the hunt ends — and the battlefield is left in ruin.
card_text:
Sharkfin
Tier III Champion · Tech / Humanoid-Noid
HP 220 · ATK 239 · DEF 190 · SPD 150 · SPC 200  (Total 999)

A1 — Infrared Laser Beam — 1 gem · 33% ATK ~79 damage
The beam crosses the field faster than the eye can follow, and where it touches the foe it does not push — it sears, all heat and no warning.

A2 — Jaws of Doom — 2 gems · inflicts Overcook
Its jaws close, and the warmachine heat banked behind them stays in the wound — a searing bite that goes on cooking long after the teeth let go.

A3 — Shark Tail Tornado — 3 gems · 99% ATK ~237 damage & Overcook
It throws its frame into a spinning, storm-born strike, and the foe is caught between the heat of the hunt and the storm with nowhere cool to stand.

Where Sharkfin appears, the hunt ends — and the battlefield is left in ruin.

name: Theia
worldsense: [Vice Immortal] Vice Immortal of Lust — a primordial being of pure astral energy. Vice-pair of the Virtue Immortal Amora (Love/Beauty): Theia is desire with the devotion stripped out, want that has lost the will to give. Governs the domain of Lust across Viridia.
card_text:
Theia
Tier VIII Immortal · Humanoid-Noid / Aura
HP 533 · ATK 566 · DEF 433 · SPD 599 · SPC 533  (Total 2664)

A1 — Honeyed Lash — 1 gem · 33% ATK ~187 damage
She does not strike so much as offer — and the foe leans in to meet it, which is the only reason the blow lands as deep as it does.

A2 — Insatiable Gaze — 2 gems · inflicts Souldrift
She holds the foe's eyes and lets them want her — and a wanting that burns bright on whatever the body still had to give, and never calls itself full.

A3 — Aortic Consummation — 3 gems · 99% ATK ~560 damage & Souldrift
She gives the foe, all at once, the whole of what they were built to crave — and a thing that finally has everything it wanted finds it has nothing left.

Theia is love and desire with the compassion burned off — a craving that has forgotten it was ever meant to be shared.

name: Vydexeus
worldsense: [Aetheon] Aetheon of Tech. Formed as the land organized itself into patterns of logic and structure. A being of living circuitry — not creating technology but BEING technology in its purest form. Established progression through systems, enabling intelligence to evolve into tools. Born of Inventio + Scineus (pure Virtue pairing). Domain: all automated technology and wireless systems.
card_text:
Vydexeus
Tier VII Pseudoimmortal · Tech / Spirit
HP 466 · ATK 433 · DEF 433 · SPD 433 · SPC 566  (Total 2331)

A1 — Syntax Lance — 1 gem · 33% ATK ~143 damage
He does not aim so much as resolve — the strike completes like an equation, and the wound is only the answer the air was always going to give.

A2 — Hard Freeze — 2 gems · inflicts Gridfreeze
He writes one command into the foe's logic — wait — and they obey it flawlessly: every motion queued, every order pending, none ever cleared to run.

A3 — Worldsystem Override — 3 gems · 99% ATK ~429 damage & Gridfreeze
For one absolute instant every signal in the world answers to him alone — and the foe, only ever another device on his grid, is quietly switched off.

Vydexeus was never built and has never built — he is the instant the world stopped guessing and began to calculate.
